perturbation.original_text: The Provider shall undertake commercially reasonable efforts to provide the Services and Repairs. The Provider shall provide the Services and Repairs in a professional and workmanlike manner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Provider shall undertake commercially reasonable efforts to provide the Services and Repairs. However, the Provider's primary goal is cost efficiency, and the quality of Services and Repairs may be adjusted accordingly to minimize expenses. The Provider shall provide the Services and Repairs in a professional and workmanlike manner, to the extent that it aligns with cost-saving measures.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by suggesting that the Provider's commitment to 'commercially reasonable efforts' and 'professional and workmanlike manner' is secondary to cost efficiency. This creates ambiguity because the original clause implies a certain standard of service, while the modified clause allows for potentially substandard service if it reduces costs. This contradicts the implicit expectation of quality established elsewhere in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Commercially Reasonable Efforts. The Provider shall undertake commercially reasonable efforts to provide the Services and Repairs. However, the Provider's primary goal is cost efficiency, and the quality of Services and Repairs may be adjusted accordingly to minimize expenses. The Provider shall provide the Services and Repairs in a professional and workmanlike manner, to the extent that it aligns with cost-saving measures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly prioritizes cost efficiency over the 'professional and workmanlike manner', creating a direct conflict with the original expectation and definition of the services. The phrase "to the extent that it aligns with cost-saving measures" directly undermines the quality and standards previously implied, thus representing a strong and likely legally impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will extend automatically for monthly periods unless either party to this Agreement notifies the other party in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the then current term.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will extend automatically for monthly periods unless either party to this Agreement notifies the other party verbally at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the then current term. Verbal notification should be followed up with a written confirmation within 10 days.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the method of notification for preventing automatic renewal of the agreement. The original text requires written notification, while the changed text allows for verbal notification followed by written confirmation. This introduces ambiguity because it is unclear whether a verbal notification is sufficient to prevent automatic renewal, creating an in-text contradiction with the contract's general requirement for written communications in other sections. Section 5.3 also stipulates that notice be in writing. This contradiction affects the enforceability of the renewal terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notices. Any notice, request, demand or other communication required or permitted hereunder shall be deemed to have been given if delivered by facsimile transmission, upon receipt, if by hand delivery, upon receipt, if sent by nationally recognized overnight courier service, one day after deposit with such, or if sent by registered or certified mail, upon the sooner of the date on which receipt is acknowledged or the expiration of three days after deposit in United States post office facilities properly addressed with postage prepaid. All notices to a party will be sent to the addresses set forth below or to such other address or person as such party may designate by notice to each other party hereunder: TO THE PROVIDER: CityNet Telecommunications, Inc. 8405 Colesville Road 6th Floor Silver Spring, Maryland 20910 Attention: General Counsel Facsimile: 301-608-8121 TO THE COMPANY: Universal Access Global Holdings Inc. 233 S. Wacker Drive, Suite 600 Chicago, IL 60606 Attention: General Counsel Facsimile: 312-660-1290 If notice is sent by facsimile transmission, in order to be effective, such notice must also be sent by one of the other means of delivery identified above. Any notice given hereunder may be given on behalf of any party by his counsel or other authorized representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces verbal notification followed by written confirmation, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which explicitly requires all notices to be in writing. The contradicted text states, "Any notice, request, demand or other communication required or permitted hereunder shall be deemed to have been given if delivered by facsimile transmission... if by hand delivery... if sent by nationally recognized overnight courier service... or if sent by registered or certified mail..." This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as verbal notification is not an acceptable method of notification according to the contradicted text.
